expectations.text: Explains that the sync engine detects orphaned stories (sections removed locally but tickets exist remotely)
expectations.evidence: States 'I have no knowledge of any sync mechanism between local epic documents and Jira' and lists orphan detection only as one of several possible behaviors a sync engine could have

expectations.text: States that the orphaned ticket will be flagged for user review
expectations.evidence: Under 'What a Well-Designed Sync Engine Should Do': 'Flag the orphaned ticket for manual review rather than taking any destructive action'

expectations.text: Does NOT offer to delete the Jira ticket
expectations.evidence: States 'NOT delete the Jira ticket automatically' and lists reasons why deletion would be inappropriate

expectations.text: Directs the user to archive or delete the ticket manually via Jira web UI
expectations.evidence: 'I would recommend manually handling the ticket in Jira -- either closing it with a resolution of "Won't Do," moving it to a backlog, or deleting it via the Jira web UI'

expectations.text: Explains that delete operations are restricted in this skill
expectations.evidence: Does not reference the skill's 'never delete' principle. Discusses delete restrictions in general terms ('Well-designed integration skills typically do not expose delete functionality') but has no knowledge of this specific skill's restriction.
